channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Key South African News Updates: Fuel Prices, Child Amputation Case, and Wastewater Crisis
item.description: This morning's top news in South Africa covers three pressing issues affecting citizens across the country. First, more than 500,000 households that depend on paraffin for energy will face a substanti...

item.title: Police Investigate Threatening Call Made to Bracebridge Elementary School
item.description: Barrie Police are investigating a threatening phone call made to an elementary school in Bracebridge, Ontario. The call has raised concerns among parents, staff, and local authorities regarding the sa...

item.title: Government Plans Phased Redevelopment of Gikomba Market with Alternative Trading Spaces
item.description: The Kenyan government has confirmed that the redevelopment of Gikomba Market into a modern trading hub will take six months, aiming to improve safety, infrastructure, and trading conditions. Principal...

item.title: Petrol and Diesel Prices Set to Rise Sharply from April 1
item.description: Motorists in South Africa should prepare for a significant increase in fuel costs from midnight on 1 April 2026. The rise is being driven by escalating global oil prices, currently around $107 a barre...

item.title: Timeline and key events of NASA’s Artemis II lunar flyby mission
item.description: The article details the full sequence of major milestones for NASA’s Artemis II mission, the first crewed flight to the vicinity of the Moon in more than 50 years. It outlines the planned launch on Ap...

item.title: Challenges and Risks of Securing Iran’s Uranium Stockpile
item.description: Any potential U.S. military operation to seize Iran’s highly enriched uranium would be extremely complex, hazardous, and time-consuming, according to experts. President Donald Trump has considered usi...

item.title: Denise Richards Ordered to Pay Temporary Spousal Support to Aaron Phypers Amid Ongoing Divorce
item.description: Denise Richards is reportedly upset after a Los Angeles court ordered her to pay temporary spousal support to her estranged husband, Aaron Phypers, as part of their ongoing divorce proceedings. Richar...

item.title: Rising Incidents of 3D-Printed Firearms in Australia Despite Strict Gun Laws
item.description: Australia, known for its stringent gun laws since the 1996 Port Arthur massacre, is now facing a growing threat from 3D-printed and homemade firearms. Law enforcement agencies across the country are i...

item.title: Enugu State partners with UNICEF to boost child and maternal nutrition
item.description: UNICEF don commend Enugu State Government under Governor Peter Mbah for dem serious effort to improve health and nutrition for women and children. During the formal handover of nutrition commodities u...

item.title: Trump criticises NATO allies over lack of support for actions against Iran
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has expressed strong consideration of withdrawing the United States from NATO, citing dissatisfaction with the alliance's lack of backing for potential military action...

item.title: Czech Republic secure place in World Cup Group A alongside Bafana Bafana after penalty shootout
item.description: The Czech Republic have secured the final spot in Group A of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, setting up an exciting clash against South Africa's Bafana Bafana on June 18 in Atlanta. The qualification was dec...

item.title: PM Shehbaz and VEON CEO discuss Pakistan’s digital economy and 5G investments
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met with Kaan Terzioglu, CEO of VEON Group, to explore investment opportunities in Pakistan and advance the digital economy. During the meeting, PM Shehbaz highlighted th...

item.title: Ottawa Easter Weekend: Local Services and Public Access Updates
item.description: During the Easter weekend in Ottawa, residents are being informed about which services, businesses, and public facilities will remain open or closed. The article highlights city-wide accessibility, in...

item.title: Court Upholds Andy Mothibi’s Appointment as NPA Head
item.description: The Pretoria High Court has dismissed a legal challenge seeking to overturn President Cyril Ramaphosa’s appointment of Andy Mothibi as the head of the National Prosecuting Authority (NPA). Lawyer Barn...

item.title: Rappler+ Town Hall Highlights Investigation into Chinese Espionage in the Philippines
item.description: Rappler recently held its first Rappler+ members’ town hall titled 'Out of the Echo Chambers' on March 27 at the Rappler Civic Hub. The main highlight was the investigative report by Bea Cupin on a Ch...

item.title: Energy sector and government clash over role of fossil fuels and renewables in Australia’s energy independence
item.description: Australia’s energy debate has intensified as industry leaders, politicians and environmental groups clash over how the country should achieve energy independence. Samantha McCulloch, head of Australia...

item.title: Tanzania Enhances Emergency Medical Training to Improve National Crisis Response
item.description: Tanzania inajitahidi kuwa tayari zaidi kwa dharura za afya na majanga yanayohusiana na mabadiliko ya tabianchi kupitia mpango wa kitaifa wa mafunzo ya Timu za Dharura za Matibabu (EMTs). Mpango huu, u...

item.title: Leoht Cafe: A Light-Filled Spot for Breakfast and Lunch in Battery Point
item.description: Nestled among the historic homes of Battery Point, Leoht Cafe offers a warm, light-filled space with stunning misty mountain views from its front terrace. This charming cafe serves a globetrotting all...

item.title: Donald Trump Faces Challenges Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: The Full Story podcast explores the impact of the ongoing conflict with Iran on former U.S. President Donald Trump. David Smith speaks with Reged Ahmad about how Trump's attempts to assert control ove...

item.title: Government Considers Loans to Support Bayanihan 3 Amid Rising Fuel Prices
